title: fixed reading status from self.attrs
body:
This is a fix for the following error:
```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/podman/domain/containers.py", line 61, in status
    return self.attrs["State"]["Status"]
           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^
TypeError: string indices must be integers, not 'str'
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *sekyHC* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [mheon](https://github.com/mheon) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

mmh, for every `testing-farm` looks like I am getting this

```
        with self.subTest("Run Container"):
            top_ctnr = self.client.containers.run(
                self.alpine_image, "/usr/bin/top", name="TestRunPs", detach=True
            )
>           self.assertEqual(top_ctnr.status, "running")
E           AssertionError: {'OciVersion': '1.2.1', 'Status': 'runnin[425 chars]00Z'} != 'running'

podman/tests/integration/test_containers.py:125: AssertionError
```

let me investigate
I confirm there's a failing test. can you reproduce it locally with `tox -e py -- -k test_container_crud`?

yup, looks like `self.attrs["State"]["Status"]` gets just the status, ie, `running`, and you are returning the whole `"State"` structure. so tests are right to fail

If you can give me a reproducer of how you got the error in the PR description I can help you with the investigation

```python
    async def list_containers(self, request):
        def sync_list():
            with PodmanClient(base_url="unix:///tmp/podman.sock") as client:
                result = []
                for c in client.containers.list(all=True):
                    result.append({
                        "Id": c.id,
                        "Name": c.name,          # container name
                        "Image": c.image.tags[0],
                        "Status": c.status,   # podman-py gives status directly
                    })
                return result

        containers = await asyncio.to_thread(sync_list)
        return web.json_response(containers)
```

This was giving me the error:

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/podman/domain/containers.py", line 61, in status
    return self.attrs["State"]["Status"]
           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^
TypeError: string indices must be integers, not 'str'
```

in order to fix it and actually get status, I had to delete ["Status"] in the library, like I did in this PR

Aha! then I know!

- here's the issue you are having:
  https://github.com/containers/podman-py/issues/446

- and here's the fix that will be released in the next versoin and merged just few mins ago:
  https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/540
  
Now, you have 2 options:

1. know the usage of list, and call reload manually as per the README. https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/README.md?plain=1#L55-L61
2. use the upstream version (or the one I am planning to releaes today or tomorrow, 5.6.0) and use `list(sparse=False)` which will reload under the hood.

to add a little bit of context, the state of containers is not retrieved automatically on each `list` call because the `podman inspect` call (which is what gets you the correct status) is expensive, so by default we don't do that.

hope that helps :)
